name: KELLNER: Google to Launch Chrome OS in '09
description: Washington Times technology columnist Mark Kellner reports on Google's announcement that Chrome OS will ship in 2009 as a cloud-centric netbook operating system. Kastner — identified here as 'chief research officer at Scott-Page, a Westport, Mass., technology research firm' and 'a 40-year veteran of the technology industry and co-founder of the noted Aberdeen Group' — emails Kellner that web apps in the cloud usually work but are limited by network bandwidth and trust in the vendor's backup and security. Kastner cites a model flaw — 'always-on Internet in emerging markets' — and notes consumers are using redundant cloud vendors (Picasa + Snapfish) to hedge against vendor failure. NEW career datapoint: Scott-Page affiliation 2009.
